Values lower than 85% are marked as Warning while values below 70% are marked as Critical. These ratings indicate that there may be anomalous situations with the involved metrics that need checking. This includes potentially missing configurations.

A low System Health score usually results from one or more low KPIs. If you identify a low score on the Overview page, start by identifying the system(s) responsible for the low value. The overall System Health score is simply the average of the individual System Health scores.

Within each system, you can easily identify which KPI is responsible for a low score. To access the Operation Details view, click on the header of a system tile under the Eligible Systems section. This detailed view provides a list of metrics used for the KPI calculations.

Keep in mind that some KPIs have a greater influence on score calculations due to their specific weights (see At a Glance: Available Scores, KPIs, and Metrics on the SAP Help Portal for all details). Higher weights are assigned to KPIs that are typically considered more important for evaluation in the "clean core operations" context. These KPIs also offer customers more opportunities to influence the metric values in the dashboard.

Let's have a look at the following example: the Performance KPI has a weight of only 10% in the System Health score calculation. Additionally, the KPI is based on two metrics with their own weights for the KPI calculation: Average Response Time (90%) and Maximum Response Time (10%). These metrics have fairly high SLAs: 4 seconds for the Average Response Time and 200 seconds for the Maximum Response Time. In contrast, the Exceptions KPI has a weight of 25%. Exceptions, such as ABAP Runtime Errors, can generally be addressed by tuning the system configuration, fixing code for custom applications, and ensuring that programs run smoothly.

To improve the value of a KPI, ensure the corresponding metrics fall below the SLAs defined for each metric in the At a Glance: Available Scores, KPIs, and Metrics topic on the SAP Help Portal. The SAP Cloud ALM for Operations applications, such as Health Monitoring, Integration & Exception Monitoring, and Job & Automation Monitoring apps, are invaluable tools. They help you identify which instances have connectivity issues, which runtime error categories occur most frequently and for which programs, and which jobs fail most often.

 

Next Steps: How to Get Started?

Step 1: Ask your administrator to assign the role Operations Dashboard Viewer to your user. The easiest way to do this is to use the User Management application in SAP Cloud ALM and scroll down to the RISE with SAP section when editing the role assignments of a user:

Note that you'll be able to see the RISE with SAP section only if you are a RISE with SAP customer.

Step 2: Make sure that the Health Monitoring and Integration & Exception Monitoring apps are configured for your SAP S/4HANA systems. Also, don't forget to activate the following categories in the Integration & Exception Monitoring app: ABAP Runtime, ABAP System Log, and ABAP Application Log. For a more detailed explanation, check out the following page: How to connect SAP S/4HANA and SAP Business Suite 7 on-premise systems to SAP Cloud ALM

Step 3: Confirm that you have at least the following ST-PI add-on version installed on your SAP S/4HANA systems: ST-PI 740 SP28.

For a detailed description of all prerequisites, see Prerequisites on the SAP Help Portal.

 

FAQ

Is this a monitoring tool?

  • No, this tool provides daily reports on RISE with SAP customers' clean core progress within the operations dimension (based on SAP's evaluation).

My System Health score is below 100%. Is this bad?

  • Ideally, the System Health score should be rated as green, meaning it has a value between 85% and 100%. The goal isn't necessarily to achieve a score of 100%. Instead, use the dashboard as a tool to identify any anomalous behavior in certain operational areas of your systems.

Which products are supported?

  • The dashboard supports SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition systems only.

Why is it not possible see and select my development systems?

  • The compliances, KPIs, and scores are calculated for your productive systems only. Within a productive system only the client marked as "productive" is considered in this dashboard.

Why does my system always show an overall health of 40% even when nothing is configured?

  • In the current state, a default compliance value is assigned when the dashboard receives no metrics due to missing configurations. For the Performance and Connectivity KPIs, the default value is zero. For the Exceptions and Background Processing KPIs, the default compliance is 100%. When both the Health Monitoring and Integration & Exceptions Monitoring apps aren't configured, the final result is 40%. This is due to the respective KPI Weights in the score calculations.
  • Here is an example calculation: 50% × 0% (Connectivity) + 25% × 100% (Exceptions) + 15% × 100% (Background Processing) + 10% × 0% (Performance) = 40%
  • A fixed System Health score of 40% is a pretty good indication that there is an issue with the configuration of the Health Monitoring or the Integration & Exception Monitoring apps.
  • Note that this behavior will change in the future by taking into account the actual status of the configuration.

Under which component can I report incidents?

  • Please use the following component for reporting incidents: SV-CLM-RMD

I can see my SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition systems but not my SAP S/4HANA (On Premise) ones. What should I do?

  • This issue is likely due to the missing value in the Customer ID field in the Landscape Management app. Check the prerequisite section described in the SAP Help Portal for further details: Prerequisites

I'm a RISE with SAP customer, but I can't see the dashboard. What should I do?

  • In certain situations, depending on the type of contract (standard vs. tailored), there may be an issue with correctly identifying customer contracts. If you're sure that you're a RISE with SAP customer but you can't see your systems, we recommend opening an incident under the following component: SV-CLM-RMD.

Can I configure my own SLAs?

  • No, you cannot. The current thresholds are configured to be the same for all RISE with SAP customers.

How were the SLAs selected?

  • It depends. For some metrics, the SLAs are chosen based on current best practices. For other metrics, like used for the  calculation of the Exceptions KPI, the thresholds were determined by conducting a statistical analysis on real productive SAP S/4HANA systems after removing significant outliers.

Where can I see the overall Operations Index?

  • The current version of the dashboard includes only one score: System Health. Since the Operations Index is calculated based on various scores, it corresponds to the System Health score in the current state of the dashboard.

 

Want to learn more about how you can leverage this powerful tool for your SAP S/4HANA systems? Visit the SAP Help Portal to access detailed resources on the RISE with SAP Methodology Operations View dashboard. There, you’ll find step-by-step guides, prerequisites and best practices to help you get the most out of this innovative tool.

entry.title: New enhancements to RISE with SAP Methodology dashboard
entry.content:
We are excited to announce significant enhancements to the RISE with SAP Methodology dashboard, building upon the application's successful release in January this year. These new updates aim to provide a more comprehensive and insightful experience, empowering you to optimize your SAP S/4HANA system and achieving a clean core. 

**Improved Software Stack Assessment** 

The updated dashboard now offers a more robust software stack assessment, emphasizing the importance of maintaining the latest SAP S/4HANA product version or feature package stack. By ensuring your system is up-to-date, you can leverage SAP-delivered innovations, mitigate security risks, and align with the clean core principles. This enhancement enables you to:  

  • Verify that your system is on the latest SAP S/4HANA product version or feature package stack 
  • Install the latest feature package stack/support package stack to stay current with SAP innovations and security patches 

**Simplified Upgrade Readiness Check** 

Upgrading to SAP S/4HANA has never been more streamlined, thanks to the SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA Upgrades. This innovative tool empowers you to assess your current system's readiness for a seamless upgrade to your desired target SAP S/4HANA release. With a single glance, the SAP Readiness Check card provides a comprehensive overview of the most critical findings from your system's latest assessment, enabling you to gauge your preparedness for the upgrade or estimate the necessary effort. 

**Enhanced Extensibility Information** 

To facilitate a better understanding of your system's extensibility, we have introduced enhanced information on separate software components using the ABAP language version ABAP for Cloud Development. This update helps you: 

  • Determine if a separate software component has been created for building extensions in Tier 1 
  • Gain insight into your system's extensibility architecture and identify potential areas for improvement 

**Unused Customer Objects: A Key Focus Area** 

The updated dashboard now provides a dedicated card to track Unused Customer Objects, which are extensions created outside of the SAP standard. Reducing the number of Unused Customer Objects is a crucial goal of the extensibility dimension. The card displays: 

  • The current share of Unused Customer Objects in your system 
  • The number of Unused Objects and Trackable Objects in your system 

**Execution of Customer Objects: A Measure of Business Process Reliance** 

To assess your efforts for future upgrades and adaptation, the dashboard now offers a card that measures the Execution of Customer Objects. This update helps you: 

  • Understand how heavily your business processes rely on executable customer objects 
  • Compare the frequency of executable customer object execution to the total execution of all executable objects in your system (including SAP, partner, and customer objects) 

**Introducing the Operations View** 

In addition to these enhancements, we are introducing a new view.  

The RISE with SAP Operations View is a dashboard available for RISE with SAP customers designed to deliver daily insights and historical data on system health-related key performance indicators, enabling organizations to efficiently align with the clean core compliance goals from the operations dimension. 

Several metrics are collected from SAP Cloud ALM applications for all your configured, productive  SAP S/4HANA systems and evaluated on a daily basis against specific thresholds. The results of these metric compliance calculations are aggregated and organized in KPIs and scores displayed in the overview and detailed pages.  

Metrics, thresholds and evaluation algorithms are shared ensuring a homogeneous compliance calculation across all RISE with SAP customers. 

***Key Features and Benefits*** 

The Operations View provides daily insights, featuring up to 30 days of historical data available until the day before, for the System Health score and its KPIs: Connectivity, Exceptions, Background Processing and Performance. 

  • Overview of System Health score for all your productive SAP S/4HANA systems (Cloud Private Edition and On Premise) 
  • System-specific view to identify and analyze metrics deviations responsible for each KPI 
  • Jump-in to standard SAP Cloud ALM applications to access the highest level of details 

Overview page of the Operations View Dashboard 

For more information about the Operations View dashboard, see https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/applicationhelp/operations-view on the SAP Help Portal and the “Introducing the new RISE with SAP Operations View Dashboard” on the SAP Community Blogs portal. 

These updates demonstrate our ongoing commitment to delivering a comprehensive and actionable RISE with SAP Methodology dashboard. By leveraging these enhancements, you can further optimize your SAP S/4HANA system, achieve a clean core, and drive business success. More details can be found on our Help Portal page: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/applicationhelp/rise-with-sap. 

If you don’t want to miss out any new feature, subscribe to the What’s New viewer:  What's New in SAP Cloud ALM | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: One of SAP TM's hidden gems: The Load Utilization Determination Component
entry.content:
I've found that in SAP Transportation Management, some very useful components don't always get the attention they deserve. This clearly applies to the Load Utilization Determination component, and this short blog post intends to rectify this somewhat.

What It Does

Simply put, this component answers an important question: "What transportation resources will I need for this set of products?" It does this by combining Package Building and Load Consolidation capabilities to run a simulation for a set of products and resources. The component doesn't actually create shipments or change your data - it just shows you what would be needed to move your products. The following result screenshot of test report /SCMTMS/TEST_PLN_LUD illustrates this, by listing the resources with utilization percentages for the packages (pallets) which were placed by the load consolidation components. The pallets themselves where created for the products using the unified package building component:

Practical Applications and Business Value

This tool helps in several business situations:

  • Resource Forecasting: Planning your transportation needs for upcoming sales periods based on expected orders, helping you secure the right carriers and vehicles ahead of time.

  • Bid Preparation/Sales order processing: When responding to customer requests, you can quickly estimate what transportation you'll need, allowing for more accurate cost estimates.

  • Scenario Planning: Test different approaches by seeing how changes in packaging or routing might affect your resource needs.

An example might look like this: A electronics distributor receives a rush order: 200 laptops, 150 monitors, and 300 accessories going to Walldorf. Before committing to same-day delivery, they run LUD:

  • Input: Product quantities + available truck types
  • Output: "You'll need 2 standard trucks at 85% capacity, or 1 large truck at 60% capacity"
  • Decision: Quote customer for large truck option with room for additional orders.

Getting Started

Setting up this component requires some basic configuration work with your planning profile (including load consolidation and unified package building settings) and master data. Note 3618303 provides basic information on how to consume this component in your custom project. The component is available for TM 9.6 and S/4HANA 1809 onwards.

Other options

Consulting solution

If a custom implementation is not an option for you, you could also take a look at the consulting solution for Transport Load Consolidation, which has implemented a process that consumes this component and integrates into sales order and delivery processes. Please refer to note 3045005 for further information on this solution.

Standard solution

As I mentioned above, we currently have no standard solution that embeds this functionality in a TM process. We do have plans in that direction, so if you have requirements or feedback around this we would love to hear from you.

entry.title: Your opportunity to Influence SAP – Customer Engagement Initiative Projects 2025-2 for SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
An exciting opportunity to influence SAP has started with the first cycle of the 2025 SAP Customer Engagement Initiative (CEI). The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative enables you as an SAP customer or partner to get early insights into SAP’s product developments and directly work with the developers to define and shape future product directions.

We are offering several interesting opportunities for customers to influence SAP’s S/4HANA solutions.

Find here the list of all projects that are relevant for the SAP S4/HANA Community or browse the whole project list here.

The projects will be open for customer registration until July 25, 2025.


SAP S4/HANA and SAP S4/HANA Cloud projects for registration:

 

ERP Cloud Delivery & Excellence

Potential Workforce Person Data Correction Tool

Test Data Variants–Driven Test Process Flow Control and Execution

Tool-Agnostic Test Automation from AI-Generated Process Variants for ERP Systems

 

ERP Digital Supply Chain Cloud

Empowering Reliability: Data-Driven Maintenance Efficiency Analysis for Strategy Optimization

Freight Rate Management for transportation processes with a low to medium complexity

Reimagine Warehouse Management with Joule

 

ERP Finance & Risk

AI assisted creation of balance validation rules AND comment proposal

Financial Reporting according to IFRS18 in S4HANA Cloud

Financial Statements in the New Review Booklets Analytical Application

 

ERP Private Cloud

Best Practice for Inspection Checklists in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud

 

ERP Public Cloud

Actual Liquidity Analysis

AI Agent for Open Item Clearing

AI-powered chatbot (Joule) for Intelligent Margin Analysis

Assess and improve your Quote-to-Cash process with meaningful Business Health KPIs

Enhancing Payment Run Experience

Optimizing Partner Solution Support in the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Ecosystem

Price Recommendation in S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition

Research: Payment Advice In Existing Business Process

Sales Contract Management - Artificial Intelligent Handling for Quantity/Value Consumption by Joule

SAP Business Suite: Agility and Innovation through seamless Integration and Harmonization

Successor for Create and Process Non-Assigned Handling Units (HU02)

 

ERP User Experience

AI-assisted error resolution and data entry for SAP Fiori apps

AI-assisted Smart Visualization of MyHome for To-Dos (situations)

 

How do I register on the Customer Influence website? A short overview about how to find and register for a CEI project can be found on influence.sap.com.

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

Thank you in advance for your participation!
